title: Creating a Web Support Time Tracking System
excerpt:
Has anyone used Monday.com to track “support block” time? We build and maintain websites. Some of our clients buy “support blocks” so they get a chunk of hours at a discounted rate, versus paying for one hour at a time. 
…

posts.author: Marge-BCM (Marge)
posts.cookedHtml:
Has anyone used Monday.com to track “support block” time? We build and maintain websites. Some of our clients buy “support blocks” so they get a chunk of hours at a discounted rate, versus paying for one hour at a time.
We are already using the time tracking column to know how long our team is spending on each ticket. But is there a way we can be actively “deducting” from a clients support block time and show how much time still available–say on a dashboard or something?
Thanks !

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Marge,

It can be done in a number of ways.

One is that you organize the support blocks as items and tickets as subitems. Your tickets have a time tracking column that you sum up in the item, then you use a formula column to deduct the used time from the support block duration. Here a screenshot of what it would look like:

2024-01-09_13-28-161229×353 59 KB

In the screenshot, the column called Used is the sum of the subitems TimeTracking columns. Min Bought is a number column were you would enter, as an example, the number of minutes of the block the client bought. Then Unused is a formula column with the syntax shown.

What if we could break free of the Formula column and write formulas that update any type of columns? What if a formula could update multiple columns at once? This is possible with the Advanced Formula Booster app.

posts.author: Marge-BCM (Marge)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Gilles, thank you for sharing. This is indeed an option. I am surprised, I guess, that there isn’t a more direct way to do this within Monday.
Thanks, again for your insight.

posts.author: GCavin (Gilles Cavin - Reinventing Formulas in monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
What did you have in mind? What would you consider to be a more direct way of doing it?
I showed the most obvious way I found, but there might be others.

posts.author: Valeriia.H (Valeriia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Marge-BCM and everybody who is looking for solution

If i got you right Time in Status app can be helpful for you. App track time the Item has spent in each workflow status. For this just

  1. Install Time in Status
  2. Choose time range (can be 1 day)
  3. Get your report!

Знімок екрана 2024-10-07 о 18.33.141920×780 84.8 KB

Also you will be able to track how much time your issue spends in each status and much more by generating 3 types of reports:

  • Time in Status Report
  • Status Count Report
  • Transition Count Report

And use charts to visualize your reports a sample of a certain number according to time range/item range, or a single item:

  • Pie Charts
  • Bar Charts
  • Area Charts

The app is also available in the format of board integration.

image1862×924 91.6 KB

You can try it on the Monday Marketplace and share your experience with us.
Hope you find this app helpful.
